plan: Step 1: Create CSS design tokens file at src/styles/tokens.css — Define CSS custom properties for colors (white bg, dark graphite, muted green accents), spacing scale, typography scale, and responsive breakpoints (--breakpoint-lg: 1200px for desktop three-column, --breakpoint-md: 768px for tablet). These tokens align with CLAUDE.md visual direction (Stripe + threat intelligence lab).

Step 2: Create the layout shell module at src/layout/shell.ts — Export a function (e.g. renderShell) that builds the semantic HTML structure: <header> with site title/branding area, <main id="dashboard"> as the content area, and <footer> with privacy trust statement. Mount this inside the existing #app div. Use semantic landmarks for accessibility.

Step 3: Create src/layout/shell.css (CSS Module or scoped CSS) — Style the shell with CSS Grid for the overall page layout (header/main/footer). For main content: use CSS Grid with `grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr)` at >=1200px (desktop three-column layout). Below 1200px, use `grid-template-columns: 1fr` so modules stack vertically (mobile). Set max-width and overflow-x: hidden on body/app to prevent horizontal scrolling. Add reduced-motion media query support.

Step 4: Update src/styles/global.css — Import tokens.css, add html/body height rules (min-height: 100vh), apply design tokens to body (background, color, font), and add overflow-x: hidden to prevent horizontal scroll on all viewports.
Step 5: Update src/app/main.ts — Import the shell module and call renderShell(app) instead of setting textContent. This wires the layout into the app bootstrap.
Step 6: Update index.html — Add semantic lang attribute (already present), ensure viewport meta is correct (already present). No other changes needed since shell renders via JS.

Step 7: Write tests at tests/us-004/responsive-shell.test.ts — Test all 6 acceptance criteria: (1) header element exists after render, (2) main content area exists, (3) footer exists, (4) CSS defines three-column grid at >=1200px breakpoint, (5) CSS defines single-column stacking below breakpoint, (6) no horizontal overflow (overflow-x hidden is set). Use Vitest with DOM assertions by rendering shell into a jsdom container.
Step 8: Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, and npm run test to verify all checks pass with zero errors.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: Render shell into jsdom and assert header, main, and footer elements exist with correct semantic tags
Unit test: Verify the shell CSS contains grid-template-columns with 3 columns inside a min-width: 1200px media query
Unit test: Verify the shell CSS contains single-column layout (grid-template-columns: 1fr) as the default/mobile style
Unit test: Verify overflow-x is set to hidden on the app container or body to prevent horizontal scrolling
Manual test: Open dev server at common viewports (375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px) and verify no horizontal scroll bar appears
Manual test: Resize browser across the 1200px breakpoint and confirm layout switches between 3-column and stacked
Run full suite: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test to confirm zero regressions
risks: CSS Modules may require Vite config changes — verify Vite handles .css imports without additional plugins (it does natively for CSS modules with .module.css naming convention)
jsdom in Vitest does not compute CSS layout — tests for responsive behavior must verify CSS rules/classes exist rather than computed layout dimensions
The three-column grid at >=1200px must accommodate future dashboard modules of varying heights — use auto-fit or explicit grid areas to avoid layout breakage when modules are added later
overflow-x: hidden on body may mask layout bugs rather than fixing root causes — pair it with max-width: 100vw and proper box-sizing to address overflow at the source
